title: as a user i want to know if podman pull found new image
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

If I do podman pull imagename:latest in script, I can't tell from return value if it found a new image or not. And output is always just list of blobs, which looks the same.

It would be necessary to know if it did downloaded new version, so that pod can be restarted and thus get to run new verion.

Look here for the use case: https://github.com/ikke-t/podman-container-systemd/blob/46d5fd08ef00fba3f6c42e34a69139a30f82a542/tasks/main.yml#L18

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman pull jacobalberty/unifi:stable

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ podman pull nextcloud:latest
Trying to pull docker://nextcloud:latest...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob f7e2b70d04ae [======================================] 21.5MiB / 21.5MiB
Copying blob 744aedb7995c [======================================] 226b / 226b
Copying blob 07afe22f8a58 [======================================] 64.3MiB / 64.3MiB
Copying blob c7bf4f31c4a4 [======================================] 238b / 238b
Copying blob b528e75732cc [======================================] 16.3MiB / 16.3MiB
Copying blob 27e7d214ded2 [======================================] 435b / 435b
Copying blob 894549c23c16 [======================================] 489b / 489b
Copying blob 9aa6d55932b2 [======================================] 11.9MiB / 11.9MiB
Copying blob 1b27fd7479e6 [======================================] 502b / 502b
Copying blob eacdac7d65c9 [======================================] 14.6MiB / 14.6MiB
Copying blob 66f1bd2ad7cf [======================================] 2.2KiB / 2.2KiB
Copying blob ee6444380c18 [======================================] 261b / 261b
Copying blob 1500f6dd9b69 [======================================] 906b / 906b
Copying blob 18560f19c8c4 [======================================] 1.5MiB / 1.5MiB
Copying blob 3da0b71d7146 [======================================] 11.7MiB / 11.7MiB
Copying blob 3820bd8e530e [======================================] 525b / 525b
Copying blob 3eb271d05479 [======================================] 539b / 539b
Copying blob fe98cc3e887d [======================================] 59.3MiB / 59.3MiB
Copying blob 9bfc0c3ccf71 [======================================] 1.9KiB / 1.9KiB
Copying blob 40e368c13955 [======================================] 917b / 917b
Copying config c1d2c267ec [======================================] 18.2KiB / 18.2KiB
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
c1d2c267ec65acd09c93ac054bfbf8344a2feed763851cbfddf72b9aa41ee272
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

e.g.

* text saying in stdout: image changed
* meaningful return value other than 0

comments.body:
FYI,

docker pull output ends with ```Status: Downloaded newer image for docker.io/ansible/awx_task:latest``` if image was refreshed, and returns 0 in both cases, for what it's worth.

```
[root@ohuska ~]# docker pull ansible/awx_web:latest

sha256:0ccca278a3219869dcd2646bb828c138cb0469753d1cb2d2bee31d38c3c4e3ab: Pulling from docker.io/ansible/awx_web
```

Thanks for opening the issue, @ikke-t! We'll look into it.

you could do:

```
podman pull $IMAGE
ctr_version=$(podman inspect --type container  --format "{{.ImageID}}" $CTR)
img_version=$(podman inspect --type image  --format "{{.Id}}" $IMAGE)

if $test ctr_version != $img_version; then
   .....
fi
```

Well we do have some precedence with podman rm and podman rmi exiting with 1 when the image does not exists.
Theoretically we could exit 0, if it pulls an image.  Exit 1 if the image already exists and no new image was pulled.  Exit 125 if there was a failure to pull.

The problem is that 1 is still a failure - there are a lot of scripts that probably start with a `podman pull $IMAGE`, and making that fail if the image exists would break them.

I wouldn't mind adding a flag to do this (exit 1 if exists), but we should not make it default.

wouldn't an exit 1 mean an error though?  That is different from `podman rm` where it is an error to specify a container that doesn't exist.
We would need something explicit like `rpm-ostree upgrade --upgrade-unchanged-exit-77`, that means return 77 if there is nothing to upgrade.

same comment at the same time :-)

I'd still suggest using `inspect`.  It is more robust if the image was already updated from another process
I am working on the progress bar messages and will link a PR soon. I am currently testing :)

I opened the necessary CI PR over at Skopeo: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/614

Feel free to have a look at the new behaviour, e.g.:
```
[valentin@nebuchadnezzar skopeo]$ sudo ./skopeo copy docker://nginx:latest containers-storage:nginx:latest
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob d9ef3a1eb792 completed: already exists
Copying blob f7e2b70d04ae completed: already exists
Copying blob 08dd01e3f3ac completed: already exists
Copying config 881bd08c0b completed
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```
I believe this is fixed in the current release.
Yes, this is working now. Thanks for catching up on the issue!
